New Claude co-work update is insane. You can now control your computer from your phone using AI. Not tomorrow, not next year, right now. Anthropic just dropped something called Claude Dispatch. And honestly, this is one of the wildest AI updates I've ever seen. You send a task from your phone, Claude does the work on your computer, and when you come back, everything is done. This changes everything. And I am going to break it all down for you right now. So, let me set the scene. You know how AI works right now, right? You sit down, you open a chat, you type something, you wait, you read, you type again. You are basically babysitting the AI the whole time. That is how it has been since day one. You have to be there. You have to watch it. You have to hold its hand. And honestly, that is kind of annoying because the whole point of AI is to save you time. But if you are sitting there the whole time, how much time are you really saving? Well, Anthropic just flipped the whole thing on its head. They launched this new feature called Claude Dispatch and it sits inside something called Claude Co-work. Now co-work was already pretty powerful. It could access your files. It could do multi-step tasks. It could basically act like a little assistant on your desktop. But it had one big problem. You had to be at your desk. You had to be right there staring at the screen. Not anymore. With Dispatch, you pair your phone to your desktop using a QR code. That is it. You scan the code and boom, your phone is now connected to your clawed session on your computer. And from that moment on, you can send tasks from anywhere. You could be on a train, you could be at a coffee shop, you could be walking your dog, does not matter. You pull out your phone, type a task, and Claude starts working on your computer back home or at your office. When you get back, the work is done sitting there waiting for you like a little gift. Now, let me explain why this is such a big deal. Because this is not just a cool feature. This is a whole new category of AI. Think about it. Up until now, AI has been a tool you use like a hammer. You pick it up, you use it, you put it down, but dispatch turns Claude into something more like a coworker. Someone who works while you are away, someone who does not need you hovering over them. You give them a task and they handle it. That is a massive shift. We are going from chatbot to co-orker. And that is not just a fancy way to say it. That is literally what is happening. Hey, if we have not met already, I am the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he is helping clients get more leads and customers, I am here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every single comment, so make sure you drop one below. Let him know what you think of this update. Seriously, he reads all of them. Okay, so let me walk you through how this actually works step by step. First, you open Claude desktop and turn on co-work mode. Then you click on dispatch. A QR code pops up on your screen. You grab your phone, scan that QR code, and now your phone is linked to your desktop session. It is one continuous conversation across both devices. So, anything you say on your phone, Claude sees it on your desktop. And anything Claude does on your desktop, you can see the results on your phone. It is like a bridge between your two devices with AI in the middle doing all the heavy lifting. Now, here is where it gets really fun. The use cases, let me give you some real examples. Say you are a content creator. You are out getting lunch and you think of a video idea. You pull out your phone, tell Claude to write a script based on some notes you have saved on your desktop. By the time you finish eating, the script is done. Or say you are a business owner. You are commuting to a meeting and you realize you need a presentation cleaned up. You send the task from your phone. Claude opens your files, fixes the presentation, and it is ready before you even walk through the door. Or or here is a simple one. You just want your downloads folder organized. You tell Claude from your phone. It sorts everything on your computer. You come home to a clean desktop. That is the kind of stuff we're talking about. Um, think about this for a second. This is basically passive work. You're not doing anything. You send a message and walk away. The AI does the work in the background. That is insane when you think about it. You are getting work done while doing other things. That is a level of productivity we have never had access to before. Not like this, not this easy. Now, I want to talk about something important. I will drop the link in the comments and description. Go check it out. Okay, so now let me talk about what this means for the bigger picture because dispatch is not just about convenience. It is a signal. It is a signal that AI companies are moving towards something much bigger. They are moving toward AI agents that work independently. Think about it. First AI could answer questions. Then it could write stuff, then it could use tools. Now it can work on your computer while you're not even there. What is the next step? The next step is AE that runs everything. You just assign work and the AI handles the rest. We are heading toward a world where you do not even need to open your laptop first. You just tell your AI what to do and it gets done. That is not science fiction. That's where we are going and dispatch is one of the first real steps in that direction. Now I do want to keep it real with you. Uh there are some limitations right now. Dispatch is only available to Max Plan users. So not everyone can use it yet. Now it is also still a research preview which means it is not the final version. They are still testing it and your desktop app has to be running for it to work. So if your computer is off, Claude cannot do anything. That makes sense, right? It needs your machine to be on to access your files and do the tasks. Also, there are some safety things to think about because Claude has access to your files. There are risks, things like accidental file deletion or prompt injection. Anthropic knows about this and they are working on it. But it is worth knowing. But here is the thing. Even with those limitations, this is still groundbreaking because the concept is what matters. The idea that you can send work to an AI from your phone and it executes on your computer, that is a new paradigm and it is only going to get better from here. More features, more access, more power. This is version one. Imagine version five. And let me put this in perspective for you. Right now, there are other AI agents out there, things like Manis, Plexity Computer, other tools trying to do similar stuff. But Claude dispatch is doing something slightly different. It is tying the mobile experience to the desktop experience in a way that feels natural. It is not trying to replace your computer. It is trying to make your computer work for you even when you are not there. That is a subtle but massive difference. And I think that is why this update matters more than people realize. So let me give you the bottom line. Claude is no longer just a chatbot. It is becoming a co-orker, a real one. One that works while you sleep, while you eat, while you commute. Dispatch removes the need to sit and use AI. It turns AI into something that works in the background. And that is the future. Asynchronous AI work. You assign it, executes, you review. Done.
